name: SMBC
attribution: Saturday Morning Breakfast Cereal by Zach Weinersmith

comics.title: Back
comics.alt: I want to be buried at the K-T boundary holding a box of brass gears marked Time Machine.

comics.title: Trash
comics.alt: Kicking myself for not teeing this up as a major kickstarter.

comics.title: Sick
comics.alt: Oddly enough, Ouroboros is also the sound a snake makes when trying to do this.

comics.title: Criminal
comics.alt: I'm so incredibly available to write this movie and 17 sequels. It's like the movie Wall Street, but with dolls.

comics.title: Experiencing
comics.alt: The flip side is once you get in the machine, you can start searching for the machine in the machine.

comics.title: Bot
comics.alt: Unrelated, on a bluesky thread we discovered that there are many philosophers whose names rhyme with Bagel, but other Jewish breads are not so favored.

comics.title: Hierarchy
comics.alt: No, it doesn't belong in the bodily needs section. If anything, it's in opposition to bodily needs.

comics.title: Tide
comics.alt: For body soap, especially private areas, you want a gentle, moisturizing Antichrist.

comics.title: Sport
comics.alt: The weird part is I'll get complaint emails about the 2037 Olympics not being possible, and not, like, the rest of this idea.

comics.title: Arp
comics.alt: How come nobody has made this? Or did they all go mad after making it, so we haven't heard?

comics.title: Terminal
comics.alt: The other thing is the robots are stingy with the porn and drugs.

comics.title: Sleep
comics.alt: The real punchline comes later when he marries a person who loves him, has a good boss, is respected by his children, works at a rewarding job for 40 years, travels the world for 25 years, and dies in painless repose, surrounded by loved ones.

comics.title: Block
comics.alt: Technically, this is literary humor.

comics.title: Joke
comics.alt: Do they still do this? When I was a kid, villains were constantly dangling from buildings and being saved.

comics.title: Shenanigans
comics.alt: Based almost entirely on something Tomer Ullman told me, and I hereby nominate him as First Dean of Shenanigans.

comics.title: Connection
comics.alt: All I'm saying is this would be the ultimate proof that God has a dirty mind.

comics.title: Future
comics.alt: I read in an econ book that panel four is a thing that actually happens, and I've been terrified ever since.

comics.title: Peace
comics.alt: Triple the median would of course be 50% more inner peace.

comics.title: Nagel
comics.alt: The best part is that it's included in a book titled Mortal Questions. Also that the original paper came out in 1969.

comics.title: Tragic
comics.alt: Later he's upset that nobody's upset.
